text.title: On Page SEO Checklist 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 5+ testable rules every On Page SEO Checklist 2026 application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Apply On Page Seo Checklist 2026 only to a single instance of p1-solo-saas-builder/Weekly content & SEO atomic push. Never batch multiple unrelated decisions through one pass.
rule.rationale: Batching turns a 1-decision artefact into a debate club. Each instance gets its own record so the rationale is traceable.

rule.statement: Every input field has a declared type and source. Free-form prose without a source citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs let LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong values. Typed + sourced inputs make the output falsifiable.

rule.statement: The output names a single accountable owner (handle, email, or org role). No 'team' or 'we' as owner.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. Future readers need a named human to chase when the artefact is stale.

rule.statement: Output carries semver-style version and last_reviewed date. Stale records (>90 days, no review) must be flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; treating them as immutable is a defect. Versioning + review-by-date forces refresh cadence.

rule.statement: Every decision in the output cites the input artefact(s) that justified it. Untraceable decisions are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without traceability, the artefact becomes opinion. Citations make the output reviewable by a future operator with no context.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
